layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market as geopolitical tensions push Bitcoin below $65K — no clear entry yet.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state right now, with the Fear & Greed index at 28. Bitcoin dropped below $65,000 after news of Iran conflict fears pushing oil toward $100 and bond yields surging. Most altcoins are bleeding even harder, with Bitcoin dominance at 56.7% meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. None of the swing trade candidates present a compelling enough setup to risk capital right now.
layman_action: Stay patient and keep cash ready. The market is fearful but hasn't reached extreme fear levels that historically mark great buying opportunities. Wait for clearer technical setups before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 9 candidates thoroughly. BCAP is at RSI 94 — extremely overbought after a 363% pump, a classic trap. GROK shows -100% change with terrible risk/reward (0.03x) — essentially dead. BONK has an oversold RSI of 29 (historically reliable signal per our credibility data with 83% win rate), but the chart bias is bearish (-0.70) with price below all MAs and -29.4% 5-day momentum — catching this falling knife is premature. ETH presents the best risk/reward profile with a bullish Three White Soldiers pattern and support at $1,900 only 1.5% away, but the geopolitical catalyst (Iran conflict, $100 oil, bond yield surge per CoinTelegraph) creates significant downside risk that makes entering now premature. BTC dominance at 56.7% also signals this is not an alt-friendly environment. I'm choosing HOLD until we see either ETH test $1,850 support or the Fear & Greed index drops to extreme fear (<20) for a higher-conviction contrarian entry.
risk_factors: Iran conflict escalation could drive further risk-off selling across all crypto assets
BTC dominance at 56.7% and rising means altcoins face additional headwinds even if BTC stabilizes
Clarity Act missing its Congressional window removes a potential positive regulatory catalyst
watch_list: Bitcoin price action at $64,000 support — a break below could trigger cascading alt liquidations
Fear & Greed index movement — a drop below 20 (extreme fear) would signal a higher-conviction contrarian entry
ETH holding $1,850-$1,900 support zone — if it holds and RSI drops below 40, that becomes a strong buy setup
market_context: The crypto market is under pressure from geopolitical risks (Iran conflict) and macro headwinds (rising oil prices, bond yields). Bitcoin dominance at 56.7% signals capital rotation away from altcoins, making this a poor environment for aggressive alt swing trades.
context_banner: Geopolitical fears and macro headwinds dominate — patience is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: Bitcoin sliding below $65K driven by Iran conflict fueling $100 oil and bond-yield surge — a macro headwind that makes entering new positions risky.
Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from weekly average of 28.4 — fearful but not yet extreme fear levels that historically mark bottoms.
BONK RSI at 29 is the only oversold candidate, but bearish chart structure (-0.70 bias) and -29% 5-day momentum suggest the knife is still falling.
ETH shows Three White Soldiers bullish pattern with 2.55x risk/reward, the best technical setup among candidates, but macro context argues for patience.
Clarity Act expected to miss Congressional summer window — removes a potential positive regulatory catalyst that could have supported prices.
